commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And all Israel heard it said that Saul had attacked a garrison of the Philistines, and that Israel also was held in abomination with the Philistines. And the people were called together to Saul at Gilgal. Hebrew, "and Israel was in abomination (stinking) with the Philistines. "See Exodus v. (Calmet) Septuagint, "despised as nothing the strangers. " Were should be omitted, as the verb is active, clamavit, in the Vulgate and Septuagint, though the Protestants have "were called "They shouted with alacrity, that Saul would lead them on to battle. (Haydock) Osiander thinks that they "exclaimed against him "for engaging them in this new war.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
